step_by_step_thinking:
The highest risk associated with the use of oral contraceptives is related to their ability to induce coagulation, leading to an increased likelihood of thromboembolic disorders. Multiple articles highlight the heightened risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) and arterial thrombosis in individuals utilizing oral contraceptives. For instance, article-19940_7 emphasizes that "venous thromboembolism (VTE) and arterial thrombosis (AT) are the most concerning side effects of the OC pill." Given this information, option C stands out as accurately representing the major complication linked with oral contraceptive usage.
